plan_metadata.name: Feature Globally Installed Azure Functions Core Tools

steps.description: Click the "close" button (red-bordered English flag icon) in the top-right corner of the Visual Studio Code welcome screen dialog to dismiss the language selection overlay.
Click in the main editor area of Visual Studio Code's workspace, specifically on the center region displaying the large semi-transparent VS Code logo where no files are currently open.
Press Ctrl+Shift+~ to open a new integrated terminal in the Visual Studio Code workspace, as indicated by the dark-themed Explorer view and the central VS Code logo.
Type the command "rm -rf ~/.fx/bin/func" into the bash terminal input field at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface, within the 'workspace' context.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code Terminal to execute the command `rm -rf ~/.fx/bin/func`.
Type the command rm -rf ~/.fx/bin/azfunc into the integrated Terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface with the Bash shell selected.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated Terminal after typing the command `rm -rf ~/.fx/bin/azfunc` to execute it.
Type text 'mkdir -p ~/.npm-global' into the integrated terminal (bash) within Visual Studio Code, targeting the command prompt under the 'workspace' folder.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to execute the command `mkdir -p ~/.npm-global`.
Type the text 'npm config set prefix "~/.npm-global"' into the integrated Terminal input at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace, within the 'workspace' folder context.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated Terminal to execute the command: `npm config set prefix "~/.npm-global"`.
Type the command `echo 'export PATH=$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$PATH' >> ~/.bashrc` into the integrated terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace.
Press the Enter key in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code, executing the partially typed command `bashrc`.
Type the text `source ~/.bashrc` into the integrated terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code interface, within the workspace window.
Press the Enter key in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code after typing `source ~/.bashrc` to execute the command.
Type the text 'npm i -g azure-functions-core-tools@4' into the integrated terminal (bash) at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace interface.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal while the command "npm i -g azure-functions-core-tools@4" is typed, to execute the installation command.
@assertion "added" exists
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" entry in the EXPLORER sidebar of Visual Studio Code, selecting the workspace item for further interaction.
Click the "Create a Agent/App" blue button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit welcome pane within Visual Studio Code, located in the left-side panel under "Get started with a guided tutorial."

Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the "New Project" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code. The click is on the list item labeled "Create your own agent by declaring instructions, actions...".

Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu under "Add an action to your declarative agent" within the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder: /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" option in the Workspace Folder selection dialog within Visual Studio Code to set the project root directory.
Click on the central Visual Studio Code welcome area, specifically on the large VS Code logo within the main workspace pane of the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" extension interface.

Press F5 to start debugging the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in the Visual Studio Code interface, with the primary sidebar open to display toolkit options under ACCOUNTS, ENVIRONMENT, DEVELOPMENT, LIFECYCLE, and UTILITY sections.
Click on the "Email or phone" input field in the Microsoft sign-in page within a web browser, precisely at the center where the cursor is indicated by a red crosshair.
Type ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft login page (https://login.microsoftonline.com) at the "Sign in" screen.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft login page after entering the email address in the Sign in form.
Click the password input field on the Microsoft login page to focus for password entry, as indicated by the red crosshair at the center of the text box under "Enter password."
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the password field on the Microsoft login page for the account, within a web browser interface.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login password entry screen in a web browser at position (638, 517), confirming the user authentication process.
Click the "Not now" button in the "Save your password?" prompt within the M365 Copilot web application interface to decline saving the entered credentials.

Click the "Yes" button in the Microsoft login dialog to confirm staying signed in on the https://login.microsoftonline.com web page. The red crosshair marks the exact button location, ensuring precise interaction with the blue "Yes" button beneath the "Stay signed in?" prompt.
Click the close (X) button at the top-right corner of the M365 Copilot browser tab to close the application window.
Click the "Explorer" icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the file explorer panel.
@assertion there is no folder "devTools".
